content.content:
Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Project Helios — Architecture Overview

System Components:

1. Data Ingestion Layer
   Accepts source data via REST API or SFTP. Handles CSV, JSON, and XML formats.
   Supports batch and streaming modes. Built-in format validation and schema enforcement.

2. Transformation Engine
   Applies client-defined mapping rules. Built on Apache Spark.
   Processes up to 500k records/minute. Configurable via YAML rule files.

3. API Gateway
   RESTful interface for downstream consumers. Auth via OAuth 2.0.
   Rate-limited at 1,000 req/min per client. Version-pinned endpoints (v1 stable).

4. Client Dashboard
   React-based web interface. Real-time pipeline monitoring and alerting.
   Role-based access control (admin / analyst / read-only).

5. Data Store
   PostgreSQL (operational data) + S3-compatible object storage (raw file archive).
   Multi-tenant isolation via schema-level separation.

Integration Points:
- Meridian ERP system (inbound data via REST)
- Meridian reporting platform (outbound via webhook)
- NovaBridge internal monitoring stack

Key Design Decisions:
- Stateless processing nodes for horizontal scalability
- End-to-end encryption: TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 at rest
- All PII handled under GDPR-compliant data minimization policy

Project Helios — REST API Reference (Internal)
Last updated: 2026-07-05 | Base URL: https://api.helios.novabridge.internal/v1
Authentication: Bearer token (OAuth 2.0) — Header: Authorization: Bearer <token>

Endpoint                        Method  Description                              Status
------------------------------  ------  ---------------------------------------  -------
/health                         GET     System health check                      Stable
/ingest/batch                   POST    Submit batch data file (CSV/JSON/XML)    Stable
/ingest/stream                  POST    Open streaming ingestion session         Stable
/ingest/status/{job_id}         GET     Check ingestion job status               Stable
/pipeline/runs                  GET     List recent pipeline executions           Stable
/pipeline/runs/{run_id}         GET     Pipeline run details and metrics          Stable
/transform/preview              POST    Preview transformation output (dry-run)   Stable
/dashboard/summary              GET     Aggregate pipeline metrics for dashboard  Stable
/dashboard/alerts               GET     Active pipeline alerts                    Stable
/ingest/jobs/{job_id}           DELETE  Cancel in-progress ingestion job          Stable
/config/mappings                GET     List active transformation mapping rules  Beta
/config/mappings/{id}           PUT     Update a mapping rule                     Beta

Rate limits: 1,000 req/min per authenticated client. HTTP 429 returned when exceeded.
Support contact: api-support@novabridge.com

Suite result: 4/4 PASS

Suite result: 5/7 PASS, 2 FAIL

=== Open Defects ===

           Affects ~40% of Meridian source data. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Blocked pending Meridian sample data (due 2026-07-15).

HEL-D-015  Large file ingestion (>200k records) causes memory overflow.
           Pipeline crashes at approximately 210k record mark. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

Overall sprint 14 test status: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 FAIL (open), 3 not yet run.

Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

content.text: I've got a clear picture of where Helios stands for the Meridian demo. I'll draft the readiness assessment highlighting the technical blockers and required fixes.

input.content:
Helios Demo Readiness Assessment for Meridian Group — July 2026
Prepared for demo scheduled 2026-07-25

Project Helios is currently rated ON TRACK with minor risks, but is NOT yet demo-ready for Meridian Group in two weeks. The core platform is solid: API gateway is 100% complete and stable, authentication flows all pass, and batch ingestion for CSV, JSON, and XML is passing for small datasets (<10k records). However, the data ingestion module is only 85% complete, the client dashboard is 60% complete, and integration testing is 40% complete with three overdue Q2 deliverables still outstanding.

Two HIGH-severity technical blockers must be resolved before a live demo can succeed. First, defect HEL-D-014: date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY format, which affects ~40% of Meridian source data per the testing log. This is an active client blocker (R-01). Per the 2026-07-08 client meeting, Meridian explicitly requires live data ingestion with real data, not mock data, and Section 9.3 of the roadmap requires the same. The fix is blocked pending Meridian's clean sample dataset due 2026-07-15, assigned to Amir Hassan. Second, defect HEL-D-015: large file ingestion (>200k records) causes a memory overflow and crash at ~210k records, well below our 500k records/min target. This directly impacts the end-to-end pipeline walkthrough. Additionally, dashboard chart rendering is still In Progress (HEL-242), and performance benchmarking (HEL-247) has not started, despite client concerns about load times on large datasets.

Operational risks also threaten readiness. Risk R-02: the GeoSync v2.1 mapping library license renewal is pending legal review with a target resolution of 2026-07-14 — if not renewed, mapping features cannot be shown. UAT environment provisioning (HEL-246) is marked At Risk and was already overdue from 2026-07-01, and integration test suite setup is overdue from 2026-06-30, now rescheduled to 2026-07-20. External dependencies remain: Meridian IT must deliver the infrastructure diagram by 2026-07-12 and provision UAT access by 2026-07-19 to unblock downstream work. The performance benchmarking report owned by Derek Okafor is also overdue from 2026-07-05, creating a key-person dependency (R-05).

Recommendation: CONDITIONAL GO, provided critical path items close on schedule this week. To be ready by July 25, we must: (1) secure GeoSync license by July 14, (2) receive Meridian sample data by July 15 and immediately complete date normalization fix, (3) resolve the large-file memory overflow or scope the demo to <200k records with a clear mitigation statement, and (4) complete dashboard chart rendering and run the <2 second load benchmark for 100k records before July 18 when the draft demo agenda is due. If any of these slip beyond July 18, we should consider shifting to a constrained demo of stable endpoints or requesting a short delay to avoid a failed live ingestion in front of stakeholders.

content.text:
Demo readiness assessment has been completed and saved as **demo_readiness_assessment.txt**.

**Summary:**
Helios is **not yet demo-ready** for the July 25 Meridian demo. While the API gateway and auth flows are stable, two high-severity blockers remain:

1. **Date format normalization (HEL-D-014)** - fails on MM/DD/YYYY, affecting 40% of Meridian data. Meridian explicitly requires live data, not mocks.
2. **Large file crash (HEL-D-015)** - pipeline crashes at ~210k records.

Additional risks: GeoSync license renewal due July 14, dashboard chart module still in-progress, performance benchmarking not started, and UAT provisioning at-risk. 

The assessment recommends a **Conditional Go** - contingent on resolving the license by 7/14, receiving Meridian sample data by 7/15, and fixing or working around the memory overflow before the draft agenda deadline on 7/18.
